title: salvia cana
intro: Salvia cana, also known as blue pitta or Mexican blue sage, is a shrubby perennial herb native to Mexico, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Salvia cana has been used to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and soothe skin irritations, making it a popular herb for promoting overall health and well-being. Herbal preparations of Salvia cana can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal benefits and can be taken internally or applied topically.
